reasoning: The Lean formalization exactly matches the informal statement. The hypothesis `∀ x : G, x ≠ 1 → Subgroup.zpowers x = ⊤` correctly encodes "every non-identity element is a generator of G" — `Subgroup.zpowers x` is the cyclic subgroup generated by x (set of integer powers), and equating it to ⊤ means x generates all of G. `[Fintype G]` captures "finite group," and `Fintype.card G` is the order of G, so `(Fintype.card G).Prime ∨ Fintype.card G = 1` is precisely "|G| is prime or |G| = 1." No hypotheses are added or dropped, no quantifiers are changed, and the conclusion is identical to the informal claim.
